12.3.1: Suggested method for examining data
Step III: you will be asked to modify all the provisional symbology.
Take each question. Read carefully and then compare it with symbols.
In the process of comparing, we suggest the following:
(i) If a basic condition is fulfilled, put a 'тЬУ' mark below the symbol.
(ii) If a basic condition is violated and there are additional attached to it put a '├Ч' mark below it.
(iii) If a basic condition is violated but there are no conditions then put a 'тЬУ or ├Ч or ?' mark below the condition.
(iv) When no information is given about the given condition put an '?' below it.
These symbols are to be carefully understood by the reader.
Illustration, we give the following example:
Ex: 1
тИЪ тИЪ тИЪ ?
тИЪ тИЪ тИЪ (1)
тИЪ ├Ч тИЪ (2)
тИЪ тИЪ (├Ч) (3)
тИЪ тИЪ ? (4)
? ? ├Ч (5)

The above example illustrates that
(1) I, II, III and IV are four basic conditions. V and VI are additional conditions. V is attached to II while VI is attached to III.
(2) In question (1), I, II, III are satisfied while IV is not.
(3) In question (2), all the basic conditions I, II, III and IV are satisfied but the additional conditions V is violated and the additional condition VI is not given.
(4) In question (3), the basic conditions I and IV are satisfied and both II and III are violated.
(5) In question (4), the basic conditions I, II and IV are satisfied and both III and VI are violated.
(6) In question (5), basic conditions I, III and IV are satisfied but II is not given information about II or V.

Step III
You have already performed step I and step II. In step I, you have written the basic and additional conditions at a suitable place. In step II, you have prepared your answer-sheet combinations. You have also become familiar with the necessary symbols to be used while examining the data. Now, in step III, read each question carefully and compare the facts given in questions and options one by one.
B. Put the appropriate mark (тЬУ or ├Ч or ? or ?, as suitable) below each condition.

Analytical Decision-Making
Whenever you obtain a ├Ч and a (├Ч) sign, do not bother to examine all facts in the question further. Just say at once 'not to be selected' and move over to the next question. This is because if a condition and an additional condition is violated, then definitely the necessary requirement is not being fulfilled. Which therefore implies that question is not suitable even if other conditions are fulfilled.

See IV
First, select your answer-choices on the following pattern:
- We select answer choices for (├Ч) if there are additional conditions that have ├Ч choice which states 'not to be selected'.
- If there is no cross line in question mark below any condition, select the question.
- If there are neither any crosses nor any question-mark, then compare the combinations with the three answer-combinations obtained in step I and accordingly select the answer-choice.
I can imagine that by now you have developed only an intuitive idea of this method, that there are many points which you have not very clearly understood. You are doubtful if this method is really faster and better than some you already know. Let me tell you, I promise you, this method after solving example jobs are still due on me.
But let me make you understand the method clearly by first example, but I convince myself and you at one time. I will use the first job I did.
Clearly, For this, a complete solution of the above example is being provided.

Ex. Solve completely the above example.
Solution:
a b c d

тИЪ тИЪ тИЪ тИЪ (2) Kishore Garg is MCA with 61% marks. His date of birth is 10th August, 1970. He has worked as a computer teacher for 3 years and obtained 69% marks in the interview.

(├Ч) тИЪ (3) Babli Sankar is M.Tech. with 58% marks. Her date of birth is 31 Dec. 1971. She has been working as a programmer for two-and-a-half years. She obtained 40 marks in the interview.

тИЪ (тЬУ) ├Ч (4) Manish Kumar is M.Sc.(Comp.Sc.) with 52%. He was born on 10.7.1968. He has been working as a computer teacher for 4 yrs. He obtained 40% in interview.

тИЪ тИЪ(тИЪ) тИЪ (5) Sudha Ranjan is M.C.A. with 56% marks. She has been working as a programmer for 3 years. Her date of birth is 12 Feb, 1969. She obtained 55% marks in the interview.
